name: Eremitas da Serra D'Ossa (PR 4) — circular route
name_original: Eremitas da Serra D'Ossa (PR 4) — percurso circular
region_intro: Hiking around Redondo, Portugal, is characterized by its diverse landscape within the Alentejo region, notably featuring the Serra d'Ossa hills. The area includes cork oak forests, agricultural plains, and serene lake environments. Trails often traverse rolling terrain, offering a mix of open views and shaded paths. Best hiking trails around Redondo The most popular hiking route is Eremitas da Serra D'Ossa (PR 4) — circular route, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route explores the varied terrain of the Serra d'Ossa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lake – Montado Cork Oak Forest loop from Camping Alentejo, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail winds through a montado cork oak forest, offering a tranquil natural experience. Local hikers also love the Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks – Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks loop from Ermida de Nossa Senhora do Monte da Virgem, a 1.8 miles (2.8 km) trail leading through the unique Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks, often completed in about 49 minutes. Hiking around Redondo is defined by the rolling hills of Serra d'Ossa, expansive cork oak forests, and scenic boardwalks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Redondo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Redondo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect to find around Redondo?
A: Hiking around Redondo offers a diverse landscape, primarily characterized by the rolling hills of the Serra d'Ossa, expansive cork oak forests (montados), and agricultural plains. You'll find a mix of open views and shaded paths, with options ranging from gentle strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Redondo?
A: Yes, Redondo offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32151568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks – Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks loop from Ermida de Nossa Senhora do Monte da Virgem&lt;/a&gt; is a short and accessible option, leading through unique boardwalks. There are 2 easy routes in total, providing gentle experiences.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Redondo area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Redondo features difficult routes that explore the varied terrain of the Serra d'Ossa. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2258167587/eremitas-da-serra-d-ossa-pr-4-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eremitas da Serra D'Ossa (PR 4) — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation. There are 2 difficult routes available.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Redondo?
A: The hiking trails around Redondo vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32151568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 1.8 miles (2.8 km) and takes less than an hour. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake – Evoramonte Castle loop from Camping Alentejo&lt;/a&gt;, can extend up to 10.9 miles (17.6 km) and take several hours to complete.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available in Redondo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the Redondo area are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2258167587/eremitas-da-serra-d-ossa-pr-4-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eremitas da Serra D'Ossa (PR 4) — circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32150429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake – Montado Cork Oak Forest loop from Camping Alentejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The trails around Redondo offer glimpses of historical and natural attractions. You might encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks&lt;/a&gt;, explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4266510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montado Cork Oak Forest&lt;/a&gt;, or even spot the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/471456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Evoramonte Castle&lt;/a&gt; from certain routes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Redondo?
A: The best time to hike in Redondo is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, with comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot in the Alentejo region, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Redondo dog-friendly?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the rural areas around Redondo, especially in the natural landscapes of the Serra d'Ossa and cork oak forests. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected areas, and to carry water for them.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Redondo?
A: The hiking experiences in Redondo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful atmosphere of the cork oak forests, the scenic views from the Serra d'Ossa, and the well-maintained paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14655887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks – Alto de São Gens loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Redondo?
A: For most hiking trails in the Redondo area, you can generally find parking available near the starting points, especially in smaller villages or designated recreational areas. It's common to find informal parking spots or small car parks at popular trailheads.

Q: Are there any wheelchair-accessible trails in Redondo?
A: While the region's terrain, particularly in the Serra d'Ossa, can be varied and uneven, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Ossa Boardwalks&lt;/a&gt; offer a more accessible option. These boardwalks provide a smoother surface, making them suitable for a wider range of visitors, though specific wheelchair accessibility may vary along the entire length.

Q: How can I find less crowded hiking trails for a more peaceful experience?
A: To find less crowded trails, consider exploring routes that venture deeper into the cork oak forests or those slightly off the main tourist paths. Early mornings or weekdays are also excellent times to enjoy the tranquility of nature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32150429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake – Montado Cork Oak Forest loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a serene natural experience away from busier spots.
tips:
The Anta da Candeeira, or Anta da Herdade da Candeeira, is one of the many megalithic remains that dot the Alentejo, more precisely in the district of Évora.

In the Portuguese lexicon, “anta” refers to the approximately five thousand megalithic monuments (or dolmens) spread across the west of the Iberian Peninsula. These enigmatic stone structures were built during the Neolithic period by communities that inherited the Cardial or Impressa cultures. Silent testimonies of a distant past, they continue to fascinate those who venture into these lands of history and stone.

This is a typical Alentejo rurality construction, consisting of: narthex, nave, chancel, baptistery and sacristy. Inside, the chancel, one can see a mural musicians with angels in female attire and tiles, the latter, replaced during the restoration, in 1996, as happened with the pavement.

At the top of the Serra d’Ossa, at an altitude of 650 metres, stands the Alto de São Gens. Among the ruins of the old chapel dedicated to the saint, memories of an occupation that dates back more than three millennia, to the Bronze and Iron Ages, echo. 

This archaeological site, shrouded in legend and symbolism, was later associated by the monks of the Congregation of São Paulo with the mythical Mount of Venus, the refuge of Viriato, the legendary Lusitanian leader who, according to tradition, descended from here to confront the Romans.

waypoints.name: Nossa Senhora do Monte da Virgem
